pi_items.source: setup_task.sh creates this directory; matches Bridge Command standard directory structure (Models/Ownship/[VesselName])
Bridge Command documentation (bridgecommand.co.uk/Doc/Current/newmodels.html) confirms boat.ini is the standard configuration file for vessel models
setup_task.sh creates this directory; syntax matches Bridge Command path conventions

task.json description specifies 'All shapes must be aligned horizontally at the mast position (X=0.0, Y=5.0)' and 'The top shape must be at Z=12.0'. setup_task.sh comment confirms 'Mast is located at X=0.0, Y=5.0, Z=12.0 (Top)'

COLREGS Annex I (International Regulations for Preventing Collisions at Sea). Multiple authoritative maritime law sources confirm: 'The vertical distance between shapes shall be at least 1.5 m' (cultofsea.com/colregs, ecolregs.com, wikipedia.org/wiki/Day_shapes)

task.json description explicitly states: 'The shapes required are: 1. Top: ball.3ds 2. Middle: diamond.3ds 3. Bottom: ball.3ds'. verifier.py (lines 90-108) checks for len(balls) >= 2 and len(diamonds) >= 1, confirming two balls and one diamond are required

privileged_info_summary: This task configures a synthetic Dredger vessel model using the real Bridge Command maritime simulator (github.com/bridgecommand/bc). The configuration requires adding Ball-Diamond-Ball day shapes per COLREGS Rule 27 (Restricted in Ability to Maneuver vessel signals). The metadata correctly specifies the mast position (X=0.0, Y=5.0) and minimum vertical spacing of 1.5 meters between shapes as mandated by COLREGS Annex I. The boat.ini file is the correct standard configuration format for Bridge Command vessel models. However, the metadata claims required_shapes are only ["ball.3ds", "diamond.3ds"] when the task actually requires the ball.3ds file to be used twice (top and bottom positions), with the complete sequence being ball-diamond-ball. Dredging operations are recognized under COLREGS as a category of vessels restricted in maneuverability.
